evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Own and maintain BigQuery data pipelines, including scheduling, monitoring, and data quality validation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Data quality validation and monitoring appear alongside fast-turnaround ad-hoc analysis and A/B testing support, giving roughly equal weight to both.
evidence.domain_risk: Support A/B testing and experimentation workflows
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Errors affect marketing/product decisions in e-commerce without regulatory or financial-reporting consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Own and maintain BigQuery data pipelines
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (BigQuery, dbt, GTM, Klaviyo) already deployed in responsibilities point to an existing, growing data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Implement event tracking via Google Tag Manager (GTM) and GA4; Build and maintain the data infrastructure that feeds CRM (Klaviyo) and paid media channels
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tools and application context (GTM, GA4, Klaviyo, Funnel) show what the role actually does day to day.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Design and maintain dashboards for Growth, Marketing, and Product teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Marketing measurement, attribution, and CRM/paid media integration place the primary audience in commercial/GTM functions.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own and maintain BigQuery data pipelines
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Ownership language over pipelines is paired with support-oriented dashboard and ad-hoc analysis work for other teams.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tooling or AI-consuming infrastructure mentioned anywhere in the posting.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality validation appears in a pipeline-ownership bullet without a dedicated ownership/framework verb, closer to a listed practice than an owned testing programme.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: monitoring and documentation protocols for data reliability
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: reliability and monitoring are a secondary concern behind delivery of dashboards and marketing measurement, with no compliance or regulatory framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Integrate data from GA4, Klaviyo, marketing platforms, and backend systems into a centralized warehouse
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new integrations (GA4, GTM) and maintaining existing BigQuery pipelines.
